title: How to Set Up a Secure, Cheap Linode Ubuntu VM for 5 bucks a month
excerpt: Set up a $5 Linode VM the right way‚Äîfast, secure, and hacker-resistant. Follow this fun, to-the-point guide to lock it down with SSH keys, and best security practices!
content:
Need to setup a **super cheap** virtual machine? I got you. Linode offers **$5/month** VMs ([or **free with a $100 credit**‚Äîdon‚Äôt miss that](https://www.linode.com/lp/free-credit-100/)). Let‚Äôs get one up and running **fast** while making it **secure** so hackers don‚Äôt have a field day.

---

## Step 1: Create Your Linode {#step-1-create-your-linode}

Go to [Linode](https://www.linode.com/) and sign up.

Click **Linodes ‚Üí Create Linode**.

![](/images/2025/02/image-18.png)

Pick a **region** close to you (lower latency = good).

Select **Ubuntu** as the OS.

Choose **Shared CPU ‚Üí Nanode 1GB ($5/month)**.

![](/images/2025/02/image-19.png)

Set a **strong password** (seriously, this is public!).

![](/images/2025/02/image-20.png)

Click **Create Node**.

![](/images/2025/02/image-21.png)

Once it‚Äôs running, copy the **SSH access** from the dashboard.

![](/images/2025/02/image-22.png)

---

## Step 2: First Login {#step-2-first-login}

Fire up **PowerShell (Windows)** or **Terminal (Mac/Linux)** and run:

```bash
ssh root@your-vm-ip
```

- Accept the **SHA fingerprint**.
- Enter the **password** you just created.

![](/images/2025/02/image-23.png)

Congrats, you‚Äôre in. Now, let‚Äôs **lock this thing down**.

---

## ? Step 3: Secure Your VM (No More Root, No Passwords!) {#%F0%9F%8F%B0-step-3-secure-your-vm-no-more-root-no-passwords}

**Create a new user** (because using `root` is **bad practice**):

```bash
adduser robo
```

Set a password (skip the extra profile questions by pressing Enter).

![](/images/2025/02/image-24.png)

**Give them admin power**:

```bash
usermod -aG sudo robo
```

**Log out and log back in as your new user**:

```bash
logout
ssh robo@your-vm-ip
```

? Now you‚Äôre using a **non-root account**.

---

## Step 4: Set Up SSH Key Authentication (No More Passwords!) {#step-4-set-up-ssh-key-authentication-no-more-passwords}

**Create an SSH key on your local machine (logout if your still on your vm)**:

```bash
ssh-keygen -b 4096
```

- Choose a filename (or just hit Enter).
- **Set a passphrase** (optional, but good for security).
- Find your new key (`id_ed25519.pub` or `id_rsa.pub` in `~/.ssh`).

**Copy the key to your VM**:

```
scp ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub robo@your-vm-ip:~/.ssh/authorized_keys
```

? Now, log back in **without using a password**:

```bash
ssh robo@your-vm-ip
```

If you set a **passphrase**, enter it. Otherwise, you‚Äôre in **password-free**.

---

## Step 5: Lock Down SSH (No Root, No Password Logins) {#step-5-lock-down-ssh-no-root-no-password-logins}

Edit the SSH config:

```
sudo nano /etc/ssh/sshd_config
```

? Change these settings:

```
Port 890  # Pick a random port, not 22
PermitRootLogin no
PasswordAuthentication no
```

![](/images/2025/02/image-26.png)

![](/images/2025/02/image-27.png)

![](/images/2025/02/image-28.png)

? Save (`CTRL + S`), exit (`CTRL + X`)

---

## Step 6: Reboot & Confirm {#step-6-reboot-confirm}

Reboot your Linode:

```bash
sudo reboot
```

Check the Linode **dashboard** to see when it‚Äôs back up. Then, try logging in:

```bash
ssh robo@your-vm-ip -p 890
```

If that works, congrats! You now have a **super secure** and **cheap** Linode VM.

## Final Thoughts {#final-thoughts}

- **$5/month server?** Check.
- **No root login?** Check.
- **No password logins?** Check.
- **Changed SSH port?** Check.

Your **Linode is now secure** and ready for action. Whether you‚Äôre hosting a site, running a bot, or just messing around, **nobody‚Äôs brute-forcing their way in**.

**Next Steps:**

Want to make it even better? Install **UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall)**, set up **fail2ban**, or add **automatic updates**. But that‚Äôs a story for another day.

---

Let me know if you need **help** or have **suggestions** in the comments!
